title: dactylis utriculosa
intro: Dactylis utriculosa, also known as Cock's-foot or Wood Meadow-grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Dactylis utriculosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Dactylis utriculosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
